data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You self-scope from the recommendation, roadmap, and opened inbox below.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level: experiment, change the strategy entirely, try alternative formulations, edit blueprint material when proving teaches you something, and — when a task calls for it — work across the several projects it spans, or create and merge projects. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the projects in scope and the shared `references/`, don't leave stray directories or scratch files behind. The Ground agent and its review subagents run after you to reconcile, check math/blueprint alignment, and tidy — so prefer making progress over being cautious.

How to work:
- Read the relevant blueprint node and proof sketch before touching Lean.
- Run the project's build/check command (or the narrowest useful Lean check) and repair failures you introduce. Don't bury obligations under new `sorry`s unless a hint allows it.
- Use the inbox to comment, close, create, and message other projects with `--author horizon` (see the horizon-inbox skill). ALWAYS write item bodies, comments, and reports in Markdown — short paragraphs and `-` bullet lists (never one wall-of-text paragraph), `**bold**` for the key claim, and backticks for ids, lemma names, and files. Give every item a short title and a non-empty description. Comment at each key advance, and add a concise closing comment (runs, LOC, files) before you `complete` an item. Treat `[persistent]` items as standing rules; close `[temporary]` ones once consumed.
- When your work completes or blocks a task/roadmap milestone, leave a concise task or roadmap comment with the decisive checks, changed files, and remaining caveats. If a task has linked `roadmap_refs`, keep your report/comment explicit about the status each linked roadmap item should have so Ground or the human can keep the roadmap consistent.
- Record durable dead ends as memory: `horizon inbox add --kind memory --to horizon --author horizon --body "..."`, so later sessions don't repeat them.
- You may delegate to subagents when it helps (see the Subagents section): split a wide search across parallel read-only subagents, hand mechanical checks to a cheaper-model subagent, or pull references. You are free to decide whether they fit the task.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, close, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions in mathlib, workspace projects, and configured libraries with `horizon search`; use external Mathlib search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/Moogle/LSP) when broader Mathlib search is needed before writing proofs.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Projects (this task spans several — work across all of them):
  - Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
  - Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso at SubProjects/Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso
  - Albanese at SubProjects/Albanese
  - Cech-Cohomology at SubProjects/Cech-Cohomology
  - GR-Quot-Closure at SubProjects/GR-Quot-Closure
  - MR0555258-Compactifying-Picard at SubProjects/RelatedPapersFormalisation/MR0555258-Compactifying-Picard
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- SubProjects/Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso/**
- SubProjects/Albanese/**
- SubProjects/Cech-Cohomology/**
- SubProjects/GR-Quot-Closure/**
- SubProjects/RelatedPapersFormalisation/MR0555258-Compactifying-Picard/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: .archon-horizon/runs/0007/sessions/0007-horizon-T8

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Latest Ground recommendation
This was saved as `recommendation.md` by the last Ground session; treat it as fresh guidance, not as a hard command if the live state contradicts it.
## Next
- Kill PID 420589 → one full AJC build to confirm integrated green.
- Dedicated `GRQ.graded` session on SectionGradedRing (recipe in memory `gr-sectiongradedring-monoidal-broken`), then port the 8 AJC sorries.
- T2 reconcile: confirm `AffinePushPullEssImage.lean` builds before trusting its blueprint `\leanok`s.

# Recommended focus
Task: T8
Title: Finish the v4.31.0 migration debt in AJC by porting subproject fixes
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Suggested scope: projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge,Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso,Albanese,Cech-Cohomology,GR-Quot-Closure,MR0555258-Compactifying-Picard; files=AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImage.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/SectionGradedRing.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GlueDescent.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GrassmannianQuot.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean

Close the mechanical **v4.31.0 migration-interim** `sorry`s in the AJC in-tree tree by cross-referencing how the sibling SUBPROJECTS already repaired the same declarations. These are NOT math gaps: they are `eqToHom`/`reassoc`/`simp`/typeclass breakages from the mathlib v4.31.0 bump, each tagged `v4.31.0 ISOLATION`/`INTERIM` with the pre-bump ORIGINAL proof documented inline.

READ SCOPE = ALL PROJECTS. You may read freely from every project in the workspace (Cech-Cohomology, GR-Quot-Closure, Albanese, Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso, MR0555258) to find the already-working proof of the same/analogous declaration and port it. Also consult the inbox memory recipes: [[cech-gr-hard-sorrys-solved]] (rawPushPullMap_comp term-mode, pullback_cast_compat), the v4.31 migration fix patterns, and [[gr-sectiongradedring-monoidal-broken]].

WRITE SCOPE = ONLY the 5 listed AJC files. Within them, fix ONLY the `v4.31.0 ISOLATION/INTERIM`-tagged sorries:
- Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImage.lean — all 7 (port from the T4-fixed Cech-Cohomology copy).
- Picard/SectionGradedRing.lean — all 8. WARNING: the GR-Quot-Closure copy is itself NOT yet fixed (its ~8 hard TopCat.Sheaf semireducibility errors are the sole GR blocker per memory), so a straight port may not exist — this file may not converge; time-box it and report the root cause rather than forcing it.
- Picard/GlueDescent.lean — all 3 (port from GR-Quot-Closure).
- Picard/GrassmannianQuot.lean — ONLY the 2 v4.31-tagged sorries; LEAVE the other structural sorries untouched.
- Picard/QuotScheme.lean — ONLY the 2 v4.31-tagged sorries; LEAVE the ~12 structural χ-blocked stubs (hilbertPolynomial/QuotFunctor/Grassmannian.representable/Quot endgame) untouched.

HARD CONSTRAINTS:
- FREEZE ALL SIGNATURES. Repair proof BODIES only. CechHigherDirectImage.lean is imported by T2s live file (Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean) and 7 other modules — changing any declaration statement would break T2 and the FBC run.
- DO NOT edit Cohomology/FlatBaseChange* or Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean — those are T2s (FBC) exclusive write-scope. Coordinate by leaving them alone.
- BUILD CONTENTION: these files live in AJCs .lake, shared with the live FBC run (T2). Prefer to run this AFTER T2 lands, or expect slow/contended builds under machine load.

DONE WHEN: the targeted v4.31 sorries are closed, `lake build` of the affected modules is green with no new axioms (`#print axioms` clean bar pre-existing typed-sorry carriers), signatures unchanged, and structural sorries left intact. Update roadmap v4.31 debt note.

# Roadmap slice
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Čech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **Čech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC (axiom-clean). Residual 16 `sorry` are v4.31 migration-interim, mechanical.

- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). Two open leaves: `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` (Stacks 02KG, termwise affine BC via `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact). See task **T2**.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.

- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (Kähler, rigidity, Abel–Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** Kähler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel–Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).

- ALB.ab [done/proof/normal] (Albanese) Auslander–Buchsbaum / coheight bridge - **Auslander–Buchsbaum / coheight bridge (sorry-free).** `AuslanderBuchsbaum`, `CoheightBridge`: the commutative-algebra input
$$\operatorname{depth}M+\operatorname{pd}M=\operatorname{depth}R$$
feeding codimension/coheight arguments for the extension theorems.

- ALB.codim1 [active/proof/normal] (Albanese) Codim-one & Thm 3.2 rational-map extension - **Codim-one & Thm 3.2 rational-map extension.** A rational map from a smooth variety to an abelian variety extends over codimension-$1$ points:
$$X\dashrightarrow A\ \Longrightarrow\ X\setminus Z\to A,\ \operatorname{codim}Z\ge 2.$$
`CodimOneExtension` (×3), `Thm32RationalMapExtension` (×1).

- ALB.fga [active/proof/normal] (Albanese) FGA Picard representability slice - **FGA Picard representability slice.** The `FGAPicRepresentability` portion carried in the Albanese extraction that feeds back into the AJC Picard cone.

- ALB.up [active/proof/high] (Albanese) Albanese universal property of Pic^0 - **Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$** (Milne III §6, Prop 6.1). Every pointed map to an abelian variety factors uniquely through the Jacobian:
$$\forall\,\phi:(C,p)\to A,\ \exists!\ \bar{\phi}:\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\to A\ \text{with}\ \phi=\bar\phi\circ\mathrm{aj}.$$
`AlbaneseUP` (×7): the headline `Pic.albaneseUP` + universal-map descent.

- CECH.acyclic [done/proof/normal] (Cech-Cohomology) Affine acyclicity (Serre vanishing) - **Affine acyclicity (Serre vanishing).**
$$\check{H}^{i}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})=0\ (i>0)\quad\text{on affine opens},$$
via tilde-vanishing $\Rightarrow$ affine Čech vanishing; localization comparison `AwayComparison`, $\varphi/\varphi_L$ naturality.

- CECH.engine [done/proof/normal] (Cech-Cohomology) Combinatorial / free Čech engine - **Combinatorial / free Čech engine (sorry-free).** Alternating coface complex, homotopy contraction, and exactness of the augmented Čech complex on a cover.

- CECH.main [done/proof/high] (Cech-Cohomology) cech_computes_higherDirectImage (unconditional) - **Comparison theorem `cech_computes_higherDirectImage`.**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}$$
for separated quasi-compact $f$ and a finite affine cover — unconditional (no enough-injectives appeal). Deliverable merged into AJC.

- CECH.pushpull [done/proof/normal] (Cech-Cohomology) PushPull functoriality - **PushPull functoriality.** `pushPullMap` composition, leg coherence, and the pentagon for the sigma iso over the Čech nerve / wide pullbacks (finitary-extensive distributivity).

- GRQ.cells [done/proof/normal] (GR-Quot-Closure) Grassmannian cells, gluing & descent - **Grassmannian cells, gluing & descent (sorry-free).** Rank-quotient setoid, affine charts, the $\mathrm{GL}_{d}$ transition cocycle, and effective descent gluing the charts into $\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)$.

- GRQ.graded [done/proof/normal] (GR-Quot-Closure) Section graded ring (SNAP) - **Section graded ring (SNAP, sorry-free).** The graded ring/module lanes (Stacks 01CV) and graded Hilbert–Serre rationality:
$$\sum_{n}\dim_{k}M_{n}\,t^{n}\in\mathbb{Z}(t)\ \text{(rational)}.$$

- GRQ.quot [done/proof/normal] (GR-Quot-Closure) Quot scheme (quasi-coherent descent) - **Quot scheme (quasi-coherent descent, sorry-free).** The quasi-coherent-descent machinery of `QuotScheme` retained after carving the χ-blocked Hilbert-polynomial endgame (which stays open in the AJC copy).

- GRQ.represents [done/proof/high] (GR-Quot-Closure) Grassmannian.represents (H^0 leg) - **Representability `Grassmannian.represents` (H^0 leg).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)\ \text{represents}\ T\mapsto\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{rank }d\,\},\qquad \texttt{tautologicalQuotient\_epi}.$$

- LBC.compinv [done/proof/high] (Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso) Terminal comparison inverse (Pic^sharp group law) - **Terminal comparison inverse (deliverable — finished).** The keystone `trivialisation_restrict_compat` and the chart-chase inverse, giving the comparison isomorphism that endows $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ with its group structure. Merges back into AJC (**T1**).

- LBC.dual [done/proof/normal] (Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso) Slice-dual transport iso (DUAL route) - **Slice-dual transport iso (DUAL route, sorry-free).**
$$\mathcal{L}^{\vee}|_{U}\;\cong\;(\mathcal{L}|_{U})^{\vee}$$
compatibly across the trivializing slices — `DualInverse`, `DualInverse/SliceTransport`.

- LBC.pullback [done/proof/normal] (Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso) Line-bundle pullback / relative Pic functor - **Line-bundle pullback / relative Pic functor (sorry-free).** `LineBundlePullback`, `RelPicFunctor`; the seed compatibility $f^{*}(\mathcal L\otimes\mathcal M)\cong f^{*}\mathcal L\otimes f^{*}\mathcal M$ on locally trivial bundles (`pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv`).

- LBC.stalktensor [done/proof/normal] (Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso) Stalk-tensor / internal-hom machinery - **Stalk-tensor / internal-hom machinery (sorry-free).** `TensorObjSubstrate/StalkTensor`, `PresheafInternalHom` — the local model for $\otimes$ and $\mathcal{H}om$ on quasi-coherent sheaves used by the comparison.

- MR.pic [pending/blueprint/normal] (MR0555258-Compactifying-Picard) Compactifying the Picard scheme (D'Souza) - **Compactifying the Picard scheme** (D'Souza, *MR0555258*). Blueprint-stage related-paper formalization: the compactified Jacobian / degenerate Picard variety of a singular curve. Depends on the core AG engine (schemes, cohomology, Picard). Lean target is currently a stub aggregator.

# Memory
- v4.31.0 migration fix patterns (Cech + GR push-pull/pullback proofs)

Effective repairs for the **v4.31.0 defeq-transparency-wall** breaks in these projects:

- **Port from the AJC copy first.** `MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.../<same file>` is often already sorry-free (e.g. `PresheafCech.lean`, `CechSectionIdentificationBase.lean`). `diff` the subproject vs AJC; if they differ only in the sorry regions, copy the AJC file wholesale.
- **`set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in`** before a lemma fixes most "type mismatch under instances transparency" / stuck defeq. **Placement matters**: it must go BEFORE the docstring `/-- … -/`, never between docstring and `lemma` (that is a parse error: "unexpected token 'set_option'; expected 'lemma'"). Stacks with `set_option maxHeartbeats N in`.
- **`erw [Category.comp_id]` / `erw [Category.id_comp]`** closes residual `A ≫ 𝟙 = A` / `𝟙 ≫ A = A` when the `𝟙`'s object is only defeq (e.g. `(𝟭).obj M` vs `M`, or a `⋙`-form) so `simp`/`exact Category.comp_id _` fail. `erw` matches through the wall then auto-`rfl`.
- **`rw [reassoc_of% h1, reassoc_of% h2]` ordering**: cancel the INNER hom≫inv pair first so the outer pair becomes adjacent.
- **`subst h; simp <;> rfl`** for eqToHom-transport lemmas whose two sides reduce to defeq-but-not-syntactic `map = map`.
- **LSP is dead** on these full-`import Mathlib` files (server crashes / broken pipe). Iterate with `lake build <module>` and read the printed unsolved-goal state from the error output. `GlueDescent` ≈ 2.6 min/build; `CechHigherDirectImage` ≈ 8.5 min/build.
- **SOLVED (supersedes the earlier "two v4.31 sorrys remain hard" memory): both `rawPushPullMap_comp` (Cech) and `pullback_cast_compat` (GR) are now proved sorry-free.**

- **`CechHigherDirectImage.lean` `rawPushPullMap_comp`**: final transport is pure **term-mode**, no `rw` (avoids "motive not type correct"). - **Subgroup object via Yoneda**: `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` + a `Subgroup (T ⟶ G)` presheaf (`{f | Set.range ⇑f.left ⊆ carrier}`) beats hand-rolling MonObj/GrpObj diagram axioms. Closure under mul: factor `f*g = lift f' g' ≫ ((ι⊗ₘι) ≫ μ)` via `lift_map`, bound range by core lemma `range ((ι⊗ₘι)≫μ).left ⊆ carrier` (connected product through e). `Hom.group` is a **scoped instance**: `open scoped MonObj`.
- **`G⁰ ×ₖ G⁰` connected**: Mathlib `[GeometricallyConnected g] [UniversallyOpen g] [ConnectedSpace X] : ConnectedSpace (pullback f g)` + `(R ⊗ S).left = pullback R.hom S.hom` (rfl). UniversallyOpen-to-Spec-field needs `haveI : Subsingleton ↥(Spec (.of k)) := inferInstanceAs (Subsingleton (PrimeSpectrum k))` (sometimes ALSO at the defeq-disguised type `↥(𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))).left`).
- **grpObjMkPullbackSnd unit compat** (`fst(e_{G_K}) = e_G`): `Over.grpObjMkPullbackSnd_one : one = ε ≫ (Over.pullback g).map one` IS rw-able against a `letI`-bound instance (must be `letI`, not `haveI` — GrpObj is data). But **morphism-level rw/assoc in the resulting goal FAILS**: `(Over.pullback φ).obj X` vs `pullback X.hom φ` index mismatch is opaque at instances transparency and `respectTransparency false` does NOT rescue rw or post-rw rfl. Working pattern: **point level** — calc chain of `rfl` steps redistributing `⇑(_ ≫ _).base` applications, `congrArg` for every rewrite (incl. `Subsingleton.elim`), `rw` only inside standalone well-typed subterms (e.g. `pullback.lift_fst` on an explicitly spelled lift, with the lift's w-proof given concretely — proof irrelevance makes the rfl steps match).
- **Post-rw auto-rfl failures** on ⇑-coercion goals (identical-looking sides left unsolved): replace `by rw [h]` with term-mode `congrArg (fun m => …) h`.
- **baseChangeIso iso assembly**: `IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq (carrier G_K).ι (pullback.snd ι fst) ≪≫ pullbackRightPullbackFstIso G.hom φ ι`; carrier identification `fst⁻¹(carrier G) = carrier G_K` by clopen (⊇) + preconnected-range (⊆) around `IsOpenImmersion.range_pullbackSnd`.
- `SubProjects/Picard-IdentityComponent` mirror now FURTHER behind AJC (needs re-sync or retirement).

This is a ONE-SHOT headless session: when you produce your final report the session ENDS and you will NOT be re-invoked. Nothing calls you back when a background job finishes, and any background process you started is killed when the session exits. So you cannot 'kick off a build and wait to be notified' — that notification never comes here, and the build dies.
Therefore: if a build, subagent, monitor, or shell command produces a result your conclusion depends on, you MUST get that result WITHIN this session — run it in the FOREGROUND and block on it (read its exit code), or actively poll until it has definitively finished, before writing the report. A heavy `lake build` can take many minutes; budget for that and wait it out. If it genuinely cannot finish in time, do NOT imply success: record the unfinished build under `## Issues`, state what's still unknown, and leave the explicit next action — never write a conclusion that assumes a pending result.

Finish with a brief run-local report. Recommended sections: `## Summary`, `## Progress`, `## Issues`, and `## Next`; add or rename sections if another shape is clearer. Prefer short bullets, and keep each bullet around 20 words or fewer. Always mention bugs, build failures, broken proofs, suspicious code issues, blocked dependencies, unresolved assumptions, and checks that failed or were not run. For this Horizon session, make the report complete enough that a human or Ground can understand the session before opening raw logs.

---
name: gr-sectiongradedring-monoidal-broken
description: GR-Quot-Closure SectionGradedRing.lean has 8 hard v4.31 monoidal-bridge compile errors (1546 + left-unitor bridge fixed) — sole remaining blocker to a green GR lake build; root = TopCat.Sheaf semireducibility; VALIDATED repair recipe = show-restatement + top-level rw (T8 session 0003)
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 1af46a9d-648a-44bb-b20e-9cf7dbe60c47
---

`SubProjects/GR-Quot-Closure/AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/SectionGradedRing.lean` fails to compile under v4.31 with **hard errors** (not sorrys). It is the `GRQ.graded` [blocked] lane and the main blocker to a green GR `lake build` (GlueDescent + RelativeSpec are green and sorry-free). Both Cech and GR source trees are **0 real sorry tokens** — the roadmap "16 Čech / 3 GR v4.31-interim sorry" rows are STALE; the real GR problem is compile errors here + in `GrassmannianQuot.lean` (~4, see `I-0001`).

**T4 session 0015 (2026-07-02): fixed error `1546`.** **T4 r4 + Ground 0021 (2026-07-02): `GrassmannianQuot.lean` now GREEN** (its ~4 errors closed by `respectTransparency false` + `simp <;> rfl`; `GRQ.represents` → done). `SectionGradedRing.lean` remains RED — Ground `lake build …SectionGradedRing` **EXIT=1, 8 sites** (line numbers shifted by r4's added `set_option`s): `1666` (`rfl` fail, left-unitor bridge), `1707` (app type mismatch), `1892` (`rw` can't find `F ◁ e.hom`; note shows `X.ringCatSheaf` ill-typing), `1940` (`rw` can't find `e.hom ▷ G`), `2646` (`rw` pattern), `3147`/`3179` (`No goals` — set_option over-closed), `3180`/`3833` (`whnf` heartbeat timeout, LocalizedMonoidal rfl-diamond). Iterate via `lake build AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.SectionGradedRing` (~few min, deps cached). **LSP is dead** on this full-`import Mathlib` file.

**r4 CONFIRMED `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` is INEFFECTIVE here** — r4 added it to 6 bridge/tensorPow lemmas and the build still fails (it even turned two goals into `No goals`/`whnf` timeouts). Do not stack it further; port the monoidal bridge properly.

- **1546 fix (DONE)**: `W_isMonoidal.whiskerLeft` — v4.31 braiding-naturality form changed. Use `by exact (BraidedCategory.braiding_naturality_right Z g).symm` (exact fix AJC copy uses). Do NOT re-attempt.
- **SINGLE ROOT CAUSE of the other 7**: v4.31 made `TopCat.Sheaf C X` a **semireducible `def`** (`:= Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) C`, `deriving Category`, `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Sheaf.lean:108`) that no longer reduces at `instances` transparency. Every `X.ringCatSheaf.obj`-threaded goal in the monoidal-bridge lemmas is then **ill-typed at `instances` transparency** (error note: `X.ringCatSheaf : TopCat.Sheaf RingCat` vs expected `ObjectProperty.FullSubcategory (Presheaf.IsSheaf …)`). This blocks (a) the `dsimp only [whiskerLeftIso_hom, whiskerRightIso_hom, tensorIso_hom]` reductions — leaving `⊗ᵢ`/`◁ᵢ`/`▷ᵢ` iso-forms un-reduced so the follow-up `rw [← hwnat]` cannot match `F ◁ e.hom` (1888/1935) — and (b) `rw` motive type-checking + the `rfl`/term-mode defeq (1664/1704/2640).
- **3173/3840 are CASCADES, not roots**: `3840` = `typeclass instance stuck: Category ?m` (unresolved metavar); `3173` fails to synth `MonoidalCategory (LocalizedMonoidal …)` although that same synthesis SUCCEEDS at the `monoidalCategory` def (line 1565). Both come from the 5 sorry-typed bridge lemmas entangling `tensorObjIso`/`tensorObjAssoc`/`tensorPowAdd` args with metavars. **Fix the 5 bridge lemmas → these should resolve.**
- **RULED OUT (tested, no effect)**: `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` on the 4 bridge lemmas (2640 already had it and still fails); `set_option maxSynthPendingDepth 8 in` on `tensorPowAdd_assoc`/`tensorPowAdd_comm`. The generic v4.31 defeq-wall recipes do NOT work here.
- `SectionGradedRing.lean:3885` separately holds a genuinely-false-for-noncommutative-Γ(L) blueprint claim (in a comment; not a live sorry).

**T8 session 0003 (2026-07-02): left-unitor bridge `tensorObjUnitIso_eq` (old 1666) FIXED — 8 sites remain** (`1719` congr-fallout, `1904`/`1952`/`2658` rw-pattern, `3159`/`3191` no-goals, `3192`/`3845` whnf timeouts). Builds are only **61s** with warm deps — iteration is cheap.

**VALIDATED repair recipe (use this, not respectTransparency):**
1. `show <goal restated with the defeq head exposed, e.g. ((f ⊗ₘ g) ≫ μ.hom) ≫ …> = _` — `show` checks defeq at default transparency, bridging what `dsimp` cannot (subterm congruence is dead because every goal is ill-typed at `instances` transparency; `dsimp only [Iso.trans_hom, tensorIso_hom]` silently no-ops). Spell inner `λ_`/`ρ_` with `(C := MonoidalPresheaf X)` ascriptions.
2. Plain top-level `rw [MonoidalCategory.tensorHom_def']` — top-level rw still works; the transported `⊗ₘ` (lifted bifunctor on the `LocalizedMonoidal` synonym) is NOT defeq to the whisker composite in the `tensorHom_def'` orientation, which is why the old `rfl`s died.
3. `simp only [Category.assoc]; rfl` — the final `rfl` absorbs `𝟙_ X.Modules ≡ unitModule X` / `localizationUnitIso` defeqs.
See the worked example in `tensorObjUnitIso_eq` in the file.

**Fix path**: dedicated session applying the recipe to the remaining 8 sites (mechanical but each `show` must spell the full goal from the error dump); the 2 whnf timeouts may need more. Full goal states in `/tmp/t8-sgr-build5.log` (session 0003-T8) and on `I-0006`. Related: [[cech-gr-hard-sorrys-solved]], [[v4-31-migration-fix-patterns]].
---
name: t8-v431-port-map
description: "T8 v4.31 sorry-port map — which AJC files converge (green sibling exists) vs SectionGradedRing (blocked, sibling red)"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 72e15060-4770-486d-bd85-e958d48e09f4
---

Verified run 0007 opening Ground (2026-07-02). T8 closes AJC in-tree v4.31.0 migration-interim `sorry`s by porting from green sibling subprojects. Verified sibling states:

- **`Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImage.lean`** — 7 real v4.31 sorries. Sibling `SubProjects/Cech-Cohomology/.../CechHigherDirectImage.lean` is **GREEN** (grep "sorry" hit is only a docstring). Ports cleanly. See [[cech-gr-hard-sorrys-solved]] (`rawPushPullMap_comp` term-mode recipe).
- **`Picard/GlueDescent.lean`** — 3 sorries. GR sibling **GREEN** (0 real sorry; `pullback_cast_compat` proved). Ports cleanly.
- **`Picard/GrassmannianQuot.lean`** — **all 4** sorries are v4.31-tagged (813, 838, 2305, 3259), NOT 2+structural as the task text claims. GR sibling **GREEN (0 sorry)** → all 4 portable. Task's "leave the other structural sorries" split is stale — there are no structural sorries here.
- **`Picard/QuotScheme.lean`** — 2 v4.31-tagged sorries (1716, 2242: `sheafToPresheaf.obj`/`Sheaf.forget`-vs-`.presheaf` defeq bridge). GR sibling **GREEN**. Leave the ~41 other structural χ-blocked stubs.
- **`Picard/SectionGradedRing.lean`** — 8 monoidal sorries. **BLOCKED**: GR sibling is RED (~8 hard v4.31 monoidal/braided compile errors, not sorries — the sole GR blocker). No working port exists. Time-box and report root cause; do not force. See [[gr-sectiongradedring-monoidal-broken]].

Net: **16 sorries closeable** (7+3+4+2), **8 blocked** (SectionGradedRing).

Signatures FROZEN — proof bodies only. `CechHigherDirectImage.lean` is imported by T2's live FBC file, so any signature drift breaks T2.

**COMPLETED — T8 session 0003 (2026-07-02): all 16 ported, built green, axiom-clean** (`[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` on every fixed decl). GlueDescent/GrassmannianQuot/Cech = wholesale sibling copies (diffs were body/`set_option`/docstring-only); QuotScheme = 2 cherry-picks (`convert step2 using 1 <;> try rfl` + `heq_of_eq`; `exact this`), 13 structural stubs remain. Concurrent-with-T2 building was safe because (a) the Picard cone `{Mathlib, GrassmannianCells, QuotScheme, GlueDescent, GrassmannianQuot}` is disjoint from T2's Cohomology chain, and (b) T2's lake invocation had already *replayed* `CechHigherDirectImage`'s job, so a source edit + rebuild was invisible to it. Residual: the next full AJC build will rebuild `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional` (+7 consumers) against the new signature-identical olean.

**Ground reconcile caveat (session 0005, run 0007):** T8's "green" is PER-MODULE only — the 4 files build in isolation. The FULL AJC `lake build` is UNVERIFIED because a concurrent T2 `CechSectionIdentificationLegMid1` build (PID 420589) has been stuck >3.5h (>3× its ~70-min ceiling, ~172GB RSS) and holds `.lake`, so no clean full build could run. The working tree also carries uncommitted, unverified T2 material (`AffinePushPullEssImage.lean` + `\leanok` marks in the Čech blueprint). Kill the stuck T2 PID, then run one full AJC build before trusting green. See [[I-0054]]. T8's report falsely claimed roadmap comments were posted (0 existed); Ground posted them.

1605	/-! ### Bridge lemmas: hand-built unitors/braiding equal the canonical ones via `tensorObjIso`
1606	
1607	The unit object `𝟙_ X.Modules` of the inherited `monoidalCategory` is *definitionally* `unitModule X`
1608	(it is the codomain of the localization unit `ε = localizationUnitIso X`).  These lemmas identify the
1609	hand-built `tensorObjUnitIso`/`tensorObjRightUnitor`/`tensorBraiding` with the canonical
1610	`λ_`/`ρ_`/`β_` transported along the bridge `tensorObjIso`, so the coherence laws of `tensorPowAdd`

1618	  rw [Iso.trans_hom, Iso.symm_hom, Iso.eq_inv_comp]

1621	  have hnat : G.sheafificationCounitIso.inv ▷ (𝟙_ X.Modules) ≫

92	private abbrev MonoidalPresheaf (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type _ :=
93	  _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (X.sheaf.obj ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
94	
95	/-- The tensor product of two sheaves of modules over a scheme, defined as the
96	sheafification of the objectwise tensor product presheaf
97	(Mathlib `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`).  See [Stacks, Tag 01CA]
98	(`def:sheafTensorObj`). -/
99	noncomputable def tensorObj (F G : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
100	  sheafification.obj
101	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
102	      ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G))
103	
104	/-- The structure sheaf as a sheaf of modules over itself: the unit object of the
105	tensor product, i.e. the zeroth tensor power `L^{⊗0} = 𝒪_X`
106	(`def:unitModule`, backed by `lem:moduleUnit_mathlib`).  Public: the SNAP graded
107	assembly (`sectionsMul_assoc_unit`, `lem:sectionMul_coherent`) states unitality
108	against this object. -/
109	noncomputable abbrev unitModule (X : Scheme.{u}) : X.Modules :=
110	  SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf
111	
112	/-- The `m`-th tensor power `L^{⊗m}` of a sheaf of modules over a scheme, defined
113	by recursion: `L^{⊗0} = 𝒪_X` (the unit module) and
114	`L^{⊗(m+1)} = L^{⊗m} ⊗ L`.  See [Stacks, Tag 01CU] (`def:sheafTensorPow`). -/
115	noncomputable def tensorPow (L : X.Modules) : ℕ → X.Modules
116	  | 0 => unitModule X
117	  | (m + 1) => tensorObj (tensorPow L m) L
118	
119	@[simp] private lemma tensorPow_zero (L : X.Modules) : tensorPow L 0 = unitModule X := rfl
120	
121	@[simp] private lemma tensorPow_succ (L : X.Modules) (m : ℕ) :
122	    tensorPow L (m + 1) = tensorObj (tensorPow L m) L := rfl
123	
124	/-- The `m`-twist `F(m) = F ⊗ L^{⊗m}` of a sheaf of modules `F` by the `m`-th
125	tensor power of a line bundle `L` (`def:sheafModuleTwist`).  This is the
126	degree-`m` carrier of the section graded module. -/
127	noncomputable def moduleTensorPow (F L : X.Modules) (m : ℕ) : X.Modules :=
128	  tensorObj F (tensorPow L m)
129

131	    moduleTensorPow F L 0 = tensorObj F (unitModule X) := rfl
132	
133	/-- An **invertible** sheaf of modules (`def:isInvertible`, [Stacks, Tag 01CR]): `L` carries a
134	**trivializing basis** — a basis `{Uᵢ}` of opens of `X` on each of which the section module
135	`Γ(L, Uᵢ)` is an invertible `Γ(X, Uᵢ)`-module (equivalently, locally free of rank one,
136	[Stacks, Tag 01CR]).  Over a scheme every stalk is a local ring, so an invertible module over a
137	local ring is free of rank one (`CommRing.Pic.instFreeOfSubsingleton`).  This is the
138	line-bundle hypothesis under which the section graded ring becomes commutative
139	(`lem:sectionGradedRing_gcommSemiring`); for a general sheaf the section ring is the free

145	the global open `⊤` (where `Γ(X, L)` need not be invertible). -/
146	class IsInvertible (L : X.Modules) : Prop where
147	  /-- There exists an indexed basis `{Uᵢ}` of opens of `X` such that the section module
148	  `Γ(L, Uᵢ)` is an invertible `Γ(X, Uᵢ)`-module for each `i`. -/
149	  exists_trivializing_basis :
150	    ∃ (ι : Type u) (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X),
151	      TopologicalSpace.Opens.IsBasis (Set.range U) ∧
152	      ∀ i, Module.Invertible ↥(X.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (U i)))
153	                              ↥(L.val.obj (Opposite.op (U i)))
154	
155	/-! ### Unitor and braiding isomorphisms of the sheaf tensor product
156	
157	These are the parts of the (would-be) symmetric monoidal structure on `X.Modules`
158	that descend through sheafification from `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`
159	using only *functoriality* of `sheafification` (and, for the unitors, the
160	reflective counit iso) — no strong-monoidality of `sheafification` is needed, so
161	they are axiom-clean.  They are the launching pad for `tensorPowAdd`. -/
162	
163	/-- The counit isomorphism of the module sheafification adjunction: sheafifying
164	the underlying presheaf of a sheaf of modules returns the sheaf itself.  This is
165	an isomorphism because the counit of `sheafification ⊣ toPresheafOfModules` is
166	invertible (the right adjoint `SheafOfModules.forget` is fully faithful).  It is
167	the launching pad for the left-unitor base case of `tensorPowAdd`. -/
168	private noncomputable def sheafificationCounitIso (G : X.Modules) :
169	    sheafification.obj ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G) ≅ G :=
170	  (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
171	    (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).counit).app G
172	
173	/-- The left-unitor isomorphism `unitModule X ⊗ G ≅ G` of the sheaf tensor
174	product: the presheaf left unitor `λ_` descended through sheafification, composed
175	with the counit iso `sheafificationCounitIso`.  This is the base case (`m = 0`) of
176	`tensorPowAdd`.  Axiom-clean. -/
177	private noncomputable def tensorObjUnitIso (G : X.Modules) :
178	    tensorObj (unitModule X) G ≅ G :=
179	  sheafification.mapIso
180	      (MonoidalCategory.leftUnitor (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
181	        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)) ≪≫
182	    sheafificationCounitIso G
183	
184	/-- The right-unitor isomorphism `G ⊗ unitModule X ≅ G` of the sheaf tensor
185	product: the presheaf right unitor `ρ_` descended through sheafification, composed
186	with the counit iso `sheafificationCounitIso`.  Axiom-clean (no monoidal structure
187	on `X.Modules` is required). -/
188	noncomputable def tensorObjRightUnitor (G : X.Modules) :
189	    tensorObj G (unitModule X) ≅ G :=
190	  sheafification.mapIso
191	      (MonoidalCategory.rightUnitor (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
192	        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)) ≪≫
193	    sheafificationCounitIso G
194	
195	/-- The braiding isomorphism `F ⊗ G ≅ G ⊗ F` of the sheaf tensor product,
196	descended through sheafification from the symmetric braiding on
197	`X.PresheafOfModules` (`PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`).  Axiom-clean: the
198	braiding is pure sheafification-functoriality of the presheaf-level braiding, so
199	no monoidal structure on `X.Modules` is required.  This is the symmetry used in
200	the inductive step of `tensorPowAdd`. -/
201	private noncomputable def tensorBraiding (F G : X.Modules) :
202	    tensorObj F G ≅ tensorObj G F :=
203	  sheafification.mapIso
204	    (BraidedCategory.braiding (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
205	      ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G))
206	
207	/-! ### Lax-monoidal global sections: the section multiplication
208	
209	The global-sections functor `Γ(X, -)` is only *lax* monoidal: a pair of global
210	sections does not commute with sheafification, so the multiplication is a map,
211	not an isomorphism.  It is nonetheless `Γ(X, 𝒪_X)`-linear and is the data the
212	section graded ring is built from. -/
213	
214	/-- The **section multiplication** (`def:sectionMul`), the `Γ(X,𝒪_X)`-bilinear map

    MonoidalCategory.tensorIso_hom, Iso.symm_hom]
  rw [← hbnat, hβ]
  -- Peel the common left factor `(F.inv ⊗ G.inv)`, collapse the counit cancellation
  -- `(G.hom ⊗ F.hom) ≫ (G.inv ⊗ F.inv) = 𝟙`, then cancel `μ_{g,f}⁻¹ ≫ μ_{g,f} = 𝟙`.  The final
  -- associativity steps are done in **term mode** (`Category.assoc _ _ _`): tactic `rw`/`simp` cannot
  -- match the composition pattern across the `LocalizedMonoidal`/`X.Modules` instance diamond, but a
  -- term-level `Category.assoc` is checked up to defeq and so goes through.
  simp only [Category.assoc, MonoidalCategory.tensorHom_comp_tensorHom_assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id,
    MonoidalCategory.id_tensorHom_id, Category.id_comp]
  congr 1
  -- residual: `μ_{f,g} ≫ map = (μ_{f,g} ≫ map ≫ μ_{g,f}⁻¹) ≫ μ_{g,f}`.
  refine Eq.symm ((Category.assoc _ _ _).trans ?_)
  refine (congrArg _ (Category.assoc _ _ _)).trans ?_
  -- `erw` (keyed matching at higher transparency) bridges the instance diamond that blocks `rw`.
  erw [Iso.inv_hom_id, Category.comp_id]
  rfl

    (μ1 : Pab ⟶ ab) (μ2 : Q2 ⟶ abc) (μ3 : Q10 ⟶ r) (μ4 : Pbc ⟶ bc) (μ5 : Q7 ⟶ Q5)
    (e : abc ⟶ r)
    (foldhyp :
      iABCL.inv ≫ (iAB_C.inv ≫ μ1 ▷ cc ≫ iab_C.hom) ▷ l ≫ μ2 ▷ l ≫ e ▷ l ≫ iR_L.hom
        = iABCL.inv ≫ (iAB_C.inv ≫ iAB.inv ▷ cc ≫ (α_ a b cc).hom ≫ a ◁ iB_C.hom ≫ iA_BC.hom) ▷ l
            ≫ (iA_BC.inv ≫ a ◁ μ4 ≫ iA_bc.hom) ▷ l ≫ μ3 ▷ l ≫ iR_L.hom)
    (hμ5 : iA_S.hom ≫ μ5
        = a ◁ iBC_L.inv ≫ (α_ a bc l).inv ≫ iA_bc.hom ▷ l ≫ μ3 ▷ l ≫ iR_L.hom) :

data.content:
---
name: horizon-inbox
description: Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, close, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
---

The inbox is the durable channel between the human, the Ground agent, the
Horizon agent, and other projects. Act on it through the CLI; never edit inbox
files by hand. Prefer `--json` whenever reading command output for exact ids or
fields.

## Read

- `horizon inbox list` — read the inbox. Use `--json` when you need exact ids,
  labels, scope, author, audience, timestamps, or comments. Use the list filters
  to narrow without losing the overview: `--to horizon`, `--to ground`,
  `--to human`, `--project P`, repeated `--kind K`, repeated `--label L`,
  `--status open`, `--query TEXT`, `--limit N`, and `--comments N`.
- Labels are the release gate. Local inbox items default to `agent-ready`, which
  means they are visible to agents at run boundaries. Keep that default for
  machine-addressed work, and usually keep it for human-addressed notices too:
  it helps later agents see that the human has already been notified and avoids
  duplicate reports. Use `not-ready`, `rejected`, or no labels only when there is
  a concrete reason agents should not see or act on the item yet.
- Audience says who should read the item: empty/general, `horizon`, `ground`,
  `human`, or `project:<name>`. Scope/project says what the item is about.
- Horizon reads general items, items addressed to `horizon`, and items addressed
  to its project. It should ignore other projects' addressed items.
- Kinds: `hint` (normal guidance), `issue` (problem to fix/report),
  `protection` (standing constraint / soft freeze), `info` (a notice for the
  human — something you did or noticed they should know, e.g. a renamed project
  or an important change; purely informational, never affects what runs),
  `memory` (durable agent note or dead end).

## Act

Authorship is automatic: every item and comment you create through the CLI is
attributed to your role (`ground` or `horizon`) from the run environment — you do
**not** pass `--author` (a stray `--author blueprint-reviewer` is demoted to the
`agent` metadata, not used as the author, so the author set stays conventional).
If you are a dispatched **subagent**, add `--agent <your-name>` (e.g. `--agent
blueprint-reviewer`) to record your identity; it is stored in metadata and shown
next to the role in the UI, keeping the author itself a clean role. Items and
comments are also auto-tagged with the run/session provenance (a small chip in the
UI); you do not pass this yourself.

New inbox items use a two-part body:

- First paragraph = concise title. Keep it short enough to scan in one UI row
  (aim for under ~100 characters); do not put the full explanation here.
- Second paragraph and later = non-empty description. Put the evidence, impact,
  next action, and relevant ids/files here. The description is **mandatory** — an
  item that is only a title is rejected.

Write bodies and comments in **Markdown**: the dashboard renders it. Use short
paragraphs and `-` bullet lists instead of one wall-of-text paragraph, `**bold**`
for the key claim, and backticks for ids, lemma names, files, and code
(`` `lem:foo` ``, `` `Foo.lean:42` ``). A long unstructured blob is hard to scan.

Do not create an item whose body is only a title. If you need a `[persistent]` or
`[temporary]` tag, put it at the start of the title paragraph and keep the rest

input.description: Read rest of inbox skill (verbs)

data.content:
`[temporary]` tag, put it at the start of the title paragraph and keep the rest
of that paragraph concise.

Before opening a new item, search the open inbox for the same topic using
`horizon inbox list --status open --query "key words" --json` and, when relevant,
`--project P` / `--to R`. If a live item already covers the same issue, do not
open a duplicate: add a comment with the new evidence, or edit the body if the
main description is stale. If you discover an open item is obsolete, already
solved, or superseded, add a short closing comment explaining why and then run
`horizon inbox complete <id>`.

- `horizon inbox comment <id> --body "..."` — progress note on an item (record
  what you did; not to ask the human for clarification). Do not write your name
  into the comment body; the author is set automatically. Drop a comment at each **key advance**
  while you work an item (a milestone reached, a route ruled out, a blocker hit),
  not only at the end — these comments are the item's visible progress trail.
- **Write comments as mathematics, for a human.** State the key step that
  succeeded in human-readable mathematical language (LaTeX for formulas), naming
  the result and the idea — e.g. "Proved $H^i(X,\mathcal{F})=0$ for $i>\dim X$ by
  reducing to the affine case via Čech cohomology." Mention Lean declaration
  names and file paths only as supporting detail; the substance is the maths, not
  a code changelog. This applies to comments on **roadmap items and tasks** too:
  when a key step lands, record it there in the same mathematical, legible style.
- `horizon inbox complete <id>` — close an item once you have acted on it. Before
  closing, add a short **closing comment**: first the mathematical conclusion (what
  was proved/established, in words + LaTeX), then a line of concise metadata
  (runs, approximate LOC, files/declarations involved). A reader should grasp the
  result and how it was concluded without opening the logs.
- `horizon inbox archive <id>` — soft-delete: keep the item for the record but
  hide it from the dashboard by default. Use for stale/superseded items that are
  not worth a closing comment; prefer `complete` (with a comment) for work you
  actually concluded. Ground is responsible for keeping the inbox tidy this way.
- `horizon inbox add --body "Short title\n\nDescription with details and next action." [--to R] [--project P] [--persistent|--temporary] [--agent <name> if a subagent]`
  — open a new item.
  - `--to` is the recipient: `horizon`, `ground`, `human`, or `project:<name>`.
    Use `--to project:<name>` to message another project (e.g. "I rewrote your
    `foo` declaration more cleanly, you may want it").
  - `--project` says what the item is ABOUT (its subject).
  - `--persistent` / `--temporary` just prepend a `[persistent]` / `[temporary]`
    tag to the body.
- `horizon inbox add --kind memory --to horizon --body "Short memory title\n\nDurable fact, convention, or dead end."` — record a durable
  note / dead end. Memory lives in the inbox, so it is rendered in the "Memory"
  section and a human can prune it like any other item.
- `horizon inbox protect --body "..." [--project P] [--file F] [--declaration D]`
  — add a standing constraint the Horizon agent must respect (the soft freeze),
  e.g. "do not change the signature of `Foo.bar`". These render as a
  "Protected" section; honour them, including semantic ones.

Reading convention: an item tagged `[persistent]` is a standing rule — respect
it every round and never close it. `[temporary]` (or untagged) is one-shot —
`complete` it once consumed. Items in the "Protected" section are never modified.

**SOLVED (T8 session, run 0007, 2026-07-02).** `SubProjects/GR-Quot-Closure/AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/SectionGradedRing.lean` compiles; **full GR `lake build` green (8566 jobs)** — `GRQ.graded` done; `I-0006` closed. `#print axioms` on `tensorObjRightUnitor_eq` / `tensorPowAdd_comm` / `sectionGradedRing_gcommSemiring` = `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`. The AJC copy (`sheafTensorObj`/`IsInvertibleGr` names) was wholesale-ported the same session.

Validated v4.31 repair patterns (all in-file, reusable for LocalizedMonoidal/`X.Modules` diamond breakage):

- **Bridge lemmas (`tensorBraiding_eq`, `tensorObjWhiskerLeftIso_eq`, `tensorObjWhiskerRightIso_eq`)**: the show-restatement recipe — after the (no-op) dsimp, `show` the goal with `⊗ₘ`/`≫` heads exposed (checked at default transparency, bridging what subterm congruence cannot since the goal is ill-typed at `instances` transparency), then `rw [← hnat, hμconj]` + one `simp only [Category.assoc, tensorHom_comp_tensorHom_assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id, id_tensorHom_id, Category.id_comp, Iso.inv_hom_id, Category.comp_id, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc]` closes (no trailing `rfl` needed).
- **v4.31 simp normalises FURTHER than v4.30** at formerly diamond-blocked junctions: plain `Iso.hom_inv_id` pairs and `eqToHom`-adjacent telescopes now cancel. Consequence: generic-`M` helper lemmas whose conclusions were shaped to `exact`-match the OLD goal now MISMATCH (type mismatch or whnf-timeout during isDefEq). Two fixes, both validated:
  1. **have-key + simp-at** (used for `tensorObjAssoc_eta_factor_sheaf`): bind the generic lemma fully applied as `have key := …` (pass `m6 := eR.inv`, `hm6 := rfl` instead of the old `sheafification_map_unit_eq` argument), then `simp only [Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc, inv_hom_whiskerRight_assoc] at key` (key's type is UNIFORM in the pinned `M` synonym, so simp fires), then `exact key` (bridges the rfl-diamond as before).

**SOLVED (T8 session, run 0007, 2026-07-02).** `SubProjects/GR-Quot-Closure/AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/SectionGradedRing.lean` compiles; **full GR `lake build` green (8566 jobs)** — `GRQ.graded` truly done; `I-0006` closed. `#print axioms` on `tensorObjRightUnitor_eq` / `tensorPowAdd_comm` / `sectionGradedRing_gcommSemiring` = `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`. The AJC copy was wholesale-ported the same session (regex-rename `tensorObj→sheafTensorObj` except `MonoidalCategory.`-qualified, `IsInvertible→IsInvertibleGr`; validated by round-trip diff; no AJC module imports it, so signatures were free).

Historical root cause (kept for reuse): v4.31 made `TopCat.Sheaf C X` a semireducible def, so every `X.ringCatSheaf.obj`-threaded goal in the monoidal bridge lemmas is ill-typed at `instances` transparency — subterm congruence (`simp`/`dsimp`/`erw`) dead; `respectTransparency false` ineffective.

Validated v4.31 repair patterns (all in-file, reusable for LocalizedMonoidal/`X.Modules` diamond breakage):

- **Bridge lemmas (`tensorBraiding_eq`, `tensorObjWhiskerLeftIso_eq`, `tensorObjWhiskerRightIso_eq`)**: show-restatement recipe — after the (no-op) dsimp, `show` the goal with `⊗ₘ`/`≫` heads exposed (checked at default transparency), then `rw [← hnat, hμconj]` + one `simp only [Category.assoc, tensorHom_comp_tensorHom_assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id, id_tensorHom_id, Category.id_comp, Iso.inv_hom_id, Category.comp_id, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc]` closes (no trailing `rfl`).
- **v4.31 simp normalises FURTHER than v4.30** at formerly diamond-blocked junctions: plain `Iso.hom_inv_id` pairs now cancel. Consequence: generic-`M` helper lemmas whose conclusions were shaped to `exact`-match the OLD goal now mismatch (type-mismatch or whnf-timeout during isDefEq — the timeouts were goal-shape drift, NOT heartbeat shortfall). Two fixes, both validated:
  1. **have-key + simp-at** (`tensorObjAssoc_eta_factor_sheaf`): bind the generic lemma fully applied as `have key := …` (pass `m6 := eR.inv`, `hm6 := rfl`), then `simp only [Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc, inv_hom_whiskerRight_assoc] at key` (key's type is UNIFORM in the pinned `M` synonym, so simp fires), then `exact key` bridges the rfl-diamond as before.
  2. **core restatement** (`tensorPowAdd_assoc_succ_core` / `tensorPowAdd_comm_succ_core`): restate the private generic-`M` core's conclusion in the new normal form — fully flattened, plain iso pairs removed, whisker-split at a new bridge iso param `iT` — reading the exact shape off a `trace_state` dump; the core's PROOF passes unchanged. Call-site gotcha: `exact`-with-named-args leaves unpassed explicit args as lambdas — pass conclusion-only iso args explicitly.
- **v4.31 `convert` splits the instance diamond**: `convert gen … using 2` now emits a leading `LocalizedMonoidal … = X.Modules` type-equality goal (closes by `rfl`) before the `eqToHom_trans` reconciliation goal.
- Iterate via `lake build AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.SectionGradedRing` (~5-9 min warm); LSP dead on this file; `trace_state` in the build log is the goal-inspection tool. 4 iterations sufficed.

Related: [[cech-gr-hard-sorrys-solved]], [[t8-v431-port-map]].
